patches.summary: Generated code contains HTTP PATCH method literals.
patches.reason: The published-library package verifier currently treats any Go file containing the string PATCH as a patch marker. These files are generated HTTP method handling, not local customizations.
patches.validated_outcome: Package verifier accepts the explicit ledger entry; no hand-authored behavior change is recorded.

patches.summary: Wire articles-publish-md --post to the captured X Articles create/update/publish sequence.
patches.reason: The generated low-level Articles endpoints worked live, but the hand-written markdown wrapper still stopped after dry-run. This X-specific patch keeps the agent-facing command aligned with the proven endpoint sequence.
patches.validated_outcome: Live smoke test created and published an X Article through articles-publish-md --post using the renamed x-twitter config namespace.

patches.summary: Convert fenced markdown code blocks to X Articles MARKDOWN atomic entities.
patches.reason: A browser edit HAR showed that X Articles does not render Draft.js code-block blocks; it uses atomic blocks bound to entity_map MARKDOWN entities.
patches.validated_outcome: HAR-derived payload shape was added to the markdown converter and covered by a focused unit test.

patches.summary: Add published-article edit lifecycle support and local cover image upload.
patches.reason: A browser edit HAR showed that changing a published Article requires ArticleEntityUnpublish before UpdateContent and ArticleEntityPublish after. The cover image flow also requires upload.x.com INIT, APPENDMULTI, FINALIZE before ArticleEntityUpdateCoverMedia.
patches.validated_outcome: Live CLI run updated and republished Article 2053246975311503364 with MARKDOWN code blocks and cover media 2053253930239266817.

patches.summary: Convert markdown image lines to X Articles MEDIA atomic entities.
patches.reason: A browser edit HAR showed body images are Draft.js atomic MEDIA entities backed by upload.x.com media IDs, distinct from the Article cover flow.
patches.validated_outcome: HAR-derived media entity binding is covered by focused unit tests for placeholder parsing and uploaded media replacement.

patches.summary: Backport Printing Press 4.2.2 client cache and Surf transport fixes into the X-customized client.
patches.reason: regen-merge reported body drift in internal/client/client.go because X adds cookie auth and absolute-host routing. The upstream template fixes therefore need surgical application instead of a blind generated overwrite.
patches.validated_outcome: Focused client tests cover auth-scoped cache keys and stable query parameter ordering.

patches.summary: Novel `users bookmarks find` command: offline keyword/author search over synced bookmarks.
patches.reason: X has no bookmark search and its API exposes no bookmark timestamp; the local store turns the bookmark graveyard into a searchable, agent-driveable working set. Reads the typed bookmarks table directly (not reimplementation).
patches.validated_outcome: Unit tests + live: keyword, --from @handle (resolved via users table), keyword+author, empty-store.

patches.summary: Removed the Enterprise-only real-time streaming endpoints (firehose/sample/compliance-stream) from CLI, MCP intents, and tools-manifest.
patches.reason: Those /stream GETs need Enterprise/Pro access and hang under request/response use; they do not fit the offline-mirror/agent vision. Kept filtered-stream rule management + account-activity. Cutting also removed the now-empty `likes` parent.

patches.summary: pp:happy-args fixtures on strict-validation reads; Examples on jobs novels; syncWarningJSON backport for valid sync_warning JSON.
patches.reason: Make the CLI pass its own Phase 5 acceptance. pp:happy-args + jobs Examples pending generator emit (cli-printing-press#2636); syncWarningJSON mirrors the merged cli-printing-press#2643 and is absorbed on next regen.
